MOVING PARTY'S SEPARATE STATEMENT IN SUPPORT OF THIS MOTION DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1345 IN THAT IT DID NOT PROVIDE ALL OF THE TEXTS NECESSARY FOR THE COURT TO REVIEW WITHOUT REFERRING TO ANOTHER DOCUMENT. SEE CRC 3.1345; ST. MARY V. SUPERIOR COURT, 223 CAL. APP. 4TH 762 (2014); MILLS V. U.S. BANK, 166 CAL. APP. 4TH 871, 892-893 (2008). THE MOTION IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY, AND THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED AS THE NOTICE OF MOTION IS DEFECTIVE IN THAT IT DID NOT SPECIFY AGAINST WHOM (PARTY OR ATTORNEY OR BOTH) THE SANCTIONS ARE SOUGHT. Motion was contested by Defendant, The contesting party did not appear in person as required per the Court's tentative ruling. The Court does not allow arguemnt and the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

3 - DEFENDANT CITY FRONT PLUMBING, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Denise Correa?S Further Discovery Responses To Request For Production Of Documents; Request For Monetary Sanctions

The unopposed motion to compel further responses re document production is granted as to Request Nos. 9, 10, 18, 19, 20, 21, 29, 31, 32, and 35. Responding Party shall provide code-compliant further responses within seven (7) days of service of notice of entry of this order. The request for sanctions is denied as the notice of motion is defective in that it did not specify against whom (party or attorney or both) the sanctions are sought.

As to the unopposed motion re special interrogatories, the motion is granted as to special interrogatory No. 20 and is denied to special interrogatories Nos. 26 and 27. is denied as the notice of motion is defective in that it did not specify against whom (party or attorney or both) the sanctions are sought. Responding Party shall provide code-compliant further responses within seven (7) days of service of notice of entry of this order.

As to the motion re requests for admission/form interrogatory 17.1, the motion appears to be frivolous as to requests for admission in that the responses to the requests for admission are code-compliant and there is no basis to compel further responses thereto. The motion appears to be a motion to compel further responses to form interrogatory 17.1 only. Moving party's Separate Statement in support of this motion does not comply with CRC 3.1345 in that it did not provide all of the texts necessary for the court to review without referring to another document. See CRC 3.1345; St. Mary v. Superior Court, 223 Cal. App. 4th 762 (2014); Mills v. U.S. Bank, 166 Cal. App. 4th 871, 892-893 (2008). The motion is denied in its entirety, and the request for sanctions is denied as the notice of motion is defective in that it did not specify against whom (party or attorney or both) the sanctions are sought.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem William Lynn at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (525/JPT)
